title: sclerostylis roxburghii
intro: Sclerostylis roxburghii, also known as the Roxburgh's grass orchid, is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its roots and rhizomes. The health benefits of Sclerostylis roxburghii include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use in wound healing and skin infections. Sclerostylis roxburghii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
